title: Incorrect Duplicate Handling in Binary Search

statement.markdown: Given a sorted array of integers, implement a binary search function that finds the first occurrence of a target number. If the target number does not exist, return -1. Ensure your function handles duplicate numbers correctly.
statement.inputFormat: A sorted array of integers (nums) and a target number (target) will be passed to your function as input.
statement.outputFormat: Your function should return the index of the first occurrence of the target number if it exists, otherwise return -1.
statement.notes: Your function should handle edge cases such as an empty array, a single-element array, or an array with duplicate numbers.

examples.explanation: The first occurrence of 3 is at index 2.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& nums, int target); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] nums, int target); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums, target): pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& nums, int target) { if (nums.empty()) return false; int left = 0, right = nums.size() - 1; while (left <= right) { int mid = left + (right - left) / 2; if (nums[mid] == target) { right = mid - 1; } else if (nums[mid] < target) { left = mid + 1; } else { right = mid - 1; } } return left < nums.size() && nums[left] == target ? left : -1; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] nums, int target) { if (nums.length == 0) return false; int left = 0, right = nums.length - 1; while (left <= right) { int mid = left + (right - left) / 2; if (nums[mid] == target) { right = mid - 1; } else if (nums[mid] < target) { left = mid + 1; } else { right = mid - 1; } } return left < nums.length && nums[left] == target ? left : -1; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums, target): if not nums: return False left, right = 0, len(nums) - 1 while left <= right: mid = left + (right - left) // 2 if nums[mid] == target: right = mid - 1 else if nums[mid] < target: left = mid + 1 else: right = mid - 1 return left < len(nums) and nums[left] == target and left or -1
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var solve = function(nums, target){ if (nums.length == 0) return false; var left = 0, right = nums.length - 1; while (left <= right) { var mid = left + (right - left) >> 1; if (nums[mid] == target) { right = mid - 1; } else if (nums[mid] < target) { left = mid + 1; } else { right = mid - 1; } } return left < nums.length && nums[left] == target ? left : -1;};

functionSignature.python: def solve(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> bool:
